meta.title: Invoice vs. Receipt: What's the Difference?
meta.description: Understand the exact legal and accounting differences between an invoice and a receipt. Learn when to issue each document to your clients.

schema.headline: Invoice vs. Receipt: What's the Difference?

blocks.title: Invoice vs. Receipt: What's the Difference?
blocks.subtitle: They are not interchangeable. Issuing the wrong document at the wrong time can ruin your cash flow and create accounting nightmares.

blocks.authorBio: Jane is a certified CPA with over 15 years of experience in small business accounting.

blocks.heading: Two Different Documents for Two Different Stages
blocks.text: The simplest way to remember the difference is timing: an invoice comes BEFORE payment, and a receipt comes AFTER payment. An invoice is a polite but legally binding request for money owed. A receipt is a confirmation that the money has been successfully collected. Using these terms interchangeably confuses clients and makes bookkeeping much harder than it needs to be.

blocks.definition: A commercial document issued by a seller to a buyer, relating to a sale transaction and indicating the products, quantities, and agreed prices for products or services that the seller had provided.
A written acknowledgment that a specified article, sum of money, or shipment of merchandise has been received. It proves ownership.

events.title: Goods Delivered
events.description: You finish the consulting project or deliver the physical product.

events.title: Invoice Issued
events.description: You send an Invoice requesting payment with 'Net 30' terms.

events.title: Payment Received
events.description: The client pays the invoice via bank transfer.

events.title: Receipt Issued
events.description: You send a Receipt confirming the balance is now $0.00.

blocks.myth: If a customer pays cash on the spot, I still need to generate an invoice first.
blocks.fact: If the transaction is immediate (like buying coffee), you skip the invoice entirely and only issue a receipt. Invoices are strictly for delayed payments (credit).

questions.question: Can an invoice also be a receipt?
questions.description: Yes! If a client pays their invoice, you can stamp the original invoice as 'PAID' or update the digital balance to $0.00. This single document now acts as both the original request and the final receipt.

questions.question: Do I legally have to provide a receipt?
questions.description: In most jurisdictions (including the US and UK), consumers have the legal right to request a receipt for any transaction, and businesses are legally obligated to provide one.

blocks.heading: Ready to send the right document?
blocks.subtext: Our free tool lets you toggle the title between 'Invoice' and 'Receipt' instantly.
